cases.notes: The critical inline-error requirement is clearly met with a specific, Ukrainian, non-accusatory, exclamation-free message ("Невірний email або пароль"). The only gap is the lack of an actionable next step (e.g., a password-reset link/suggestion).

The captured evidence is inconclusive on whether a distinct error message actually rendered after submit versus only the pre-existing static password hint being visible. Per the CRITICAL-ambiguity-graded-down rule this fails; needs a produce() capture that waits longer / asserts the error state text explicitly.
Redirect to /login with no flash of protected content is clearly evidenced. Miss: no returnTo/redirect param preserved, so the user's intended destination is lost across login.
Inline, Ukrainian, specific, non-accusatory message ("Email вже зареєстровано"). Miss: no suggested next step (login/reset) alongside the message.
Clear, warm, actionable Ukrainian empty state. Miss: references "these filters" generically rather than naming the specific selected city/topic.

Warm, Ukrainian, exclamation-free tooltip + comments-section prompt explaining login is required to give warmth. Miss: no inline clickable CTA at the point of friction (only the always-present header "Увійти" link).

Real finding: "years" is a free-text field with zero format/range validation. A backwards range ("2025-1990") is accepted verbatim with HTTP 201. No validation exists at all — scope gap, not just a UX defect.

Dedicated, Ukrainian, actionable empty state. Misses: generic "these filters" wording, and tone reads procedural rather than warm for this emotionally-charged feature; no "create a request" CTA surfaced.

Form is correctly blocked and the message is Ukrainian/exclamation-free, but it's a generic combined "city, description, contact email" banner, not inline on the description field, and is misleading (claims contactEmail is missing when it was pre-filled from the logged-in user).
Form is blocked client-side, message is Ukrainian and professional, but it's a page-level banner combining title+text, not inline on the title field, and doesn't isolate "title is required".
Strong on-brand empty state ("Тут поки тихо") with clear CTA. Miss: no icon/illustration, just typographic treatment.

Real bug found by this case: the field-specific validation detail was in English, and the frontend's getErrorMessage() never surfaces the `details` object at all — only a generic "Перевірте заповнення полів" banner reaches the user, with no constraint or count shown. A backend fix (Ukrainian @Size message + single-violation surfacing in the top-level error) is implemented in this session pending a backend restart to take effect; re-run this case after restart.

Submission behavior is correct (reason is intentionally optional, no forced validation, Ukrainian success confirmation, non-punitive tone) but the reason textarea has no placeholder/helper copy encouraging the reporter to add context before submitting blank.

Non-admin is silently redirected to the home page with zero flash or leakage of admin structure. Silent redirect (no explicit message) is a defensible security pattern, costing only a small deduction.
